full_name: Ministry of Education and Child Care
blurb: Leads B.C.'s K-12 public and independent school systems and the child care sector — from StrongStartBC early learning through graduation.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Every child in B.C. can access a K-12 education that fits their family
goals.description: The ministry oversees B.C.'s public school districts, independent schools, online learning and homeschooling so families can choose the K-12 option that works for their child, and supports the safety, inclusion and cultural belonging of every student.

supporting.name: Find a school in your community
supporting.desc: Search for public and independent schools and contact your local school district or francophone education authority.
supporting.users: Parents and caregivers of school-age children, new residents

supporting.name: Register your child for kindergarten or elementary school
supporting.desc: Register a school-age child with a public school district's neighbourhood school or catchment-area school.
supporting.users: Parents and guardians of children starting school

channels.label: Local school or school district office

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.3: Eligibility to enrol in an educational program

supporting.name: Enrol in online learning for Kindergarten to Grade 12
supporting.desc: Enrol with a Provincial or District Online Learning School to take a full or partial course load online, by web conferencing, teleconferencing or correspondence.
supporting.users: Students in Grades K-12, especially Grades 8-12

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.1: Online learning and online learning school definitions

supporting.name: Register your child as a homeschooler
supporting.desc: Parents who deliver their child's entire educational program at home must register the child with a public school principal or an independent school authority by September 30 each year.
supporting.users: Parents and guardians choosing to homeschool

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.13: Registration of children educated at home
Independent School Act  -  s.10: Registration of home schooled children

main_services.name: Choose an independent or alternative school

supporting.name: Establish a new independent school
supporting.desc: Prospective school authorities apply through an 8-step process (expression of interest, application, interview, pre-certification, interim certification and external evaluation) to open a B.C.-certified independent school.

legislation.full_name: Independent School Act  -  s.4 and Schedule: Classification of independent schools

supporting.name: Get funding as a certified independent school
supporting.desc: Certified independent school authorities receive per-student operating grants based on their Group 1-4 classification and per-student operating cost.

legislation.full_name: Independent School Act  -  s.12: Grant; s.13: Special purpose grants

main_services.name: Support your child's safety and wellbeing at school

supporting.name: Get support for school bullying, safety or inclusion concerns
supporting.desc: erase (expect respect & a safe education) helps students, families, educators and community partners report concerns and find supports for safe, caring and inclusive school communities.

supporting.name: Get support for a child with additional learning needs
supporting.desc: Inclusive education funding and program supports help students with disabilities or diverse abilities access a standard educational program alongside their peers.
supporting.users: Students with support needs and their families

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.82: Standard educational program, including individual education plans

supporting.name: Access Indigenous education programs and supports
supporting.desc: Programs, curriculum resources and staff support the success of Indigenous students and help all students learn about Indigenous cultures, languages and history.
supporting.users: Indigenous students and families, all students and educators

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Students progress through school and graduate prepared for what's next
goals.description: The ministry maintains each student's education record and Personal Education Number, sets graduation requirements, and helps students move on to work or post-secondary study.

supporting.name: Get your Personal Education Number (PEN)
supporting.desc: Request the unique nine-digit Personal Education Number assigned to students in B.C.'s education system, needed to view exam results and order transcripts.

supporting.name: Update the personal information on your student record
supporting.desc: Former students can update the name or personal details linked to their Personal Education Number so future transcripts show current information.

main_services.name: Graduate and plan your next step

supporting.name: Order a high school transcript or certificate
supporting.desc: View, order and send official B.C. or Yukon secondary school transcripts and certificates through the StudentTranscripts Service (STS).

supporting.name: Meet B.C. graduation requirements for the Dogwood Diploma
supporting.desc: Complete the 80 credits and provincial graduation assessments needed to earn the B.C. Certificate of Graduation (Dogwood Diploma), including French Immersion and Francophone Dual Dogwood pathways.

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.168(6)(a): British Columbia Certificate of Graduation

supporting.name: Get an Adult Graduation Diploma
supporting.desc: Learners 18 or older can combine credits earned at secondary and post-secondary schools toward a B.C. Adult Graduation Diploma (Adult Dogwood).

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.168(6)(b): British Columbia Adult Graduation Diploma

supporting.name: Apply for a K-12 scholarship, grant, bursary or award
supporting.desc: Provincial scholarship and awards programs recognize graduating students' academic standing and personal achievement.

supporting.name: Access dual credit courses toward high school and post-secondary credit
supporting.desc: Take approved post-secondary courses while in secondary school to earn credit toward both a Dogwood Diploma and a post-secondary credential.

supporting.name: Get education support as a student in government care
supporting.desc: Additional educational planning, advocacy and support to help children and youth in government care stay engaged in school and succeed academically.
supporting.users: Children and youth in government care

supporting.name: Get certified to teach in B.C. schools
supporting.desc: Teacher certification through the Teacher Regulation Branch is required to teach in B.C. public schools and most independent schools, including pathways for out-of-province and internationally trained teachers.
supporting.users: Aspiring, out-of-province and internationally trained teachers

legislation.full_name: Teachers Act  -  Part 4: Certification and Letters of Permission (s.30 Issuance of certificate)

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Families can access affordable, quality early learning and child care
goals.description: Through ChildCareBC, the ministry helps families find licensed child care and reduces what they pay for it, and supports free early learning through StrongStart BC.

main_services.name: Find and pay for child care

supporting.name: Find licensed child care in your community
supporting.desc: Search the child care map for licensed facilities by location, distance, program type, hours of operation and services offered.
supporting.users: Parents and caregivers seeking child care

relationships.service: Open a licensed child care facility

supporting.name: Get help finding child care through a resource and referral centre
supporting.desc: Child Care Resource and Referral Centres offer clear information, support and referrals to families and child care providers looking for care.

channels.label: Child Care Resource and Referral Centres

supporting.name: Apply for the Affordable Child Care Benefit
supporting.desc: Monthly payment that helps eligible families, with a pre-tax income of up to $111,000, cover the cost of licensed or registered child care.

supporting.name: Get automatic child care fee reductions
supporting.desc: Families using a child care provider enrolled in the Child Care Fee Reduction Initiative automatically save on fees without having to apply.

supporting.name: Attend a $10 a Day ChildCareBC Centre
supporting.desc: Families attending a participating $10 a Day ChildCareBC Centre pay no more than $200 a month per child; providers apply to join, families do not need to apply.

supporting.name: Get child care cost help as a young parent
supporting.desc: Parents under age 25 finishing high school may qualify for help paying for child care through the Young Parent Program.

supporting.name: Attend a free StrongStart BC early learning program
supporting.desc: Drop-in, play-based early learning program with stories, music and art for children from birth to age five and their parent or caregiver, led by a qualified early childhood educator.
supporting.users: Parents and caregivers of children aged 0-5

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  s.1: Early learning program definition

main_services.name: Access inclusive and Indigenous child care supports

supporting.name: Get inclusive child care supports for a child with extra needs
supporting.desc: Funding and program supports help children with extra support needs participate in fully inclusive licensed child care settings.
supporting.users: Families of children with support needs

supporting.name: Access Indigenous child care programs
supporting.desc: Distinctions-based, culturally safe child care and wraparound services developed with Indigenous people and partners.

supporting.name: Make a complaint about a licensed child care facility
supporting.desc: Report concerns about the health, safety or well-being of a child at a licensed child care facility to the local health authority for investigation.

channels.label: Make a complaint about a licensed facility

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Licensing and compliance of community care facilities

relationships.service: Open a licensed child care facility

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: B.C.'s child care sector is safe, staffed and growing
goals.description: The ministry certifies early childhood educators, licenses child care facilities, and funds new spaces, maintenance and wages so ChildCareBC can keep growing.

main_services.name: Work as an early childhood educator

supporting.name: Get certified as an early childhood educator
supporting.desc: Apply through the My ECE Registry digital platform for the ECE or ECE Assistant certificate required to work in a licensed child care facility in B.C.
supporting.users: Aspiring early childhood educators, including out-of-province and international applicants

legislation.full_name: Child Care Licensing Regulation  -  Early childhood educator qualification requirements

relationships.service: Get a criminal record check through the Criminal Records Review Program

supporting.name: Renew your ECE certificate
supporting.desc: Certified early childhood educators renew their ECE or ECE Assistant certificate through the ECE Registry to keep working in licensed care.

supporting.name: Validate an early childhood educator's certificate
supporting.desc: Confirm an ECE or ECE Assistant's certification status online using their first name, last name or registration number.

supporting.name: File a complaint about an early childhood educator
supporting.desc: Report concerns about the character, skill or conduct of a certified ECE or ECE Assistant to the ECE Registry's investigations team.

main_services.name: Operate a licensed child care facility

supporting.name: Open a licensed child care facility
supporting.desc: Apply for a community care licence to operate a child care facility, meeting Child Care Licensing Regulation requirements for health, safety, staffing and facility design.

channels.label: Open a licensed child care facility

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Licensing of community care facilities
Child Care Licensing Regulation  -  Facility, health, safety and staffing requirements

relationships.service: Find licensed child care in your community
Make a complaint about a licensed child care facility
Screen employees and volunteers as an organization

supporting.name: Get child care operating funding
supporting.desc: Ongoing per-space funding helps licensed and Registered Licence-Not-Required child care providers keep spaces open and deliver quality, affordable care.
supporting.users: Licensed and registered child care providers

supporting.name: Apply for child care space creation or maintenance funding
supporting.desc: Start-Up Grants help create new licensed child care spaces, and the ChildCareBC Maintenance Fund helps providers maintain existing facilities.
supporting.users: Child care operators, non-profits, local governments

supporting.name: Get wage enhancement funding for early childhood educators
supporting.desc: Funding helps licensed child care operators increase wages for qualified early childhood educators on staff, supporting workforce recruitment and retention.

foundational_services.name: K-12 curriculum, assessment and graduation policy development
foundational_services.desc: Development and maintenance of B.C.'s curriculum, provincial graduation assessments, and the Graduation Program Policy Guide and Handbook of Procedures used by school districts and independent schools.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, school districts, independent school authorities

legislation.full_name: School Act  -  Ministerial authority over educational programs

foundational_services.name: Child care facility licensing inspection and compliance
foundational_services.desc: Health authorities inspect and monitor licensed child care facilities for compliance with the Child Care Licensing Regulation on behalf of the ministry, and publish inspection reports.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, regional health authorities

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Inspection and enforcement

foundational_services.name: Independent school inspection and evaluation
foundational_services.desc: The Inspector of Independent Schools conducts external evaluations and inspections to verify independent schools continue to meet Independent School Act and classification requirements.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, Inspector of Independent Schools

legislation.full_name: Independent School Act  -  s.3: External evaluation committees
